summary: 17th-century Dutch Golden Age painter of lively, comedic genre scenes. Known for The Feast of St. Nicholas, The Merry Family, Beware of Luxury (In Luxury, Look Out!), The Dancing Couple. Also worked as brewer and innkeeper. Co-founded Guild of St. Luke in Leiden.

works.name: The Feast of St. Nicholas
works.description: Boy in gold/yellow dress delighted by gift of doll while older brother cries over birch rod. Children teach a cat to dance. Domestic gift-giving scene. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Child in gold dress receives doll while older brother cries over birch rod; domestic gift-giving on St. Nicholas's Day
examples: a child in a gold dress has received all the gifts during the title celebration
a boy is crying because the only gift he's received is a lousy birch rod

clues.clue: In the Gallery of Honour at the Rijksmuseum alongside The Merry Family
examples: is displayed in the Gallery of Honour at the Rijksmuseum

works.name: The Merry Family
works.description: Large family drinking, smoking, and playing instruments around a dinner table. Man holds fiddle and raises glass.

clues.clue: Large family drinking, smoking, playing instruments; man holds fiddle and raises glass
examples: depicted several young and old people playing instruments around a dinner table
a man holding a fiddle and raising a glass

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Dutch Golden Age painter of lively genre scenes. Brewer and innkeeper by profession. Co-founded Guild of St. Luke in Leiden. Other works: Beware of Luxury (woman balances wine glass in man's lap), The Dancing Couple, The Drawing Lesson, The Christening Feast, Doctor's Visit scenes, Children Teaching a Cat to Dance. Miro's Dutch Interiors series inspired by him.

clues.clue: Careers as brewer and innkeeper influenced his paintings of tavern scenes
examples: This artist of The Christening Feast and The Drawing Lesson painted multiple tavern scenes inspired by his profession as an innkeeper

clues.clue: Beware of Luxury (In Luxury, Look Out!): woman balances wine glass in man's lap; legs reverse the proverb 'it takes strong legs to carry wealth'
examples: a woman balancing a glass of wine in the lap of a man whose unusually positioned legs reverse the proverb

clues.clue: Multiple doctor's visit scenes: doctor in dramatic costume visiting lovesick/pregnant women
examples: This artist painted multiple theater-inspired scenes of a doctor clothed in dramatic costume visiting lovesick or pregnant young women

clues.clue: The Drawing Lesson: teacher uses plaster nude on desk to instruct child and young woman
examples: A teacher uses the plaster nude on his desk to instruct a child and young woman

clues.clue: Inspired Joan Miro's Dutch Interiors series
examples: inspired two 'interiors' in a series by Joan Miro

clues.clue: Co-founded Guild of St. Luke in Leiden
examples: Steen co-founded a guild dedicated to this saint in Leiden

comprehensive_summary:
Jan Steen was a 17th-century Dutch Golden Age painter known for lively, comedic genre scenes. He worked as a brewer and innkeeper, which influenced his many tavern and domestic scenes.

The Feast of St. Nicholas is his most frequently clued work, depicting a child in a gold dress delighted by a doll while an older brother cries over receiving only a birch rod. Children teach a cat to dance in another section. It hangs in the Gallery of Honour at the Rijksmuseum.

The Merry Family shows a large family drinking, smoking, and playing instruments around a table, with a man holding a fiddle and raising a glass. Beware of Luxury (In Luxury, Look Out!) shows a woman balancing wine in a man's lap, reversing the proverb about strong legs. He painted multiple scenes of doctors visiting lovesick or pregnant women and The Drawing Lesson where a teacher uses a plaster nude.

He co-founded the Guild of St. Luke in Leiden and inspired Joan Miro's Dutch Interiors series.
